summary: Session 3A — Vendor Payables + QR Codes backend regression. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_session_3a_extended.py with 34 tests covering all items from the review request. Combined with existing test_session_3a.py: 41/41 pass. Verified: (1) QR JSON+PNG for all 4 kinds with correct deep-link templates, (2) bogus kind returns 404 on both info and PNG, (3) refresh endpoint regenerates a valid PNG, (4) admin /stats returns all required keys, (5) /ledger filter by status/modality/vendor_id, (6) admin modality endpoint writes payment_modality into db.partners by ObjectId (verified directly in Mongo), (7) invalid modality 400, unknown vendor 404, (8) statements/generate default = previous calendar month and idempotent on second run for same period (BAD period -> 400), (9) /statements list filters, (10) approve flips db.partners.payment_modality and marks request approved, deny does NOT change modality, double-decision 400, unknown id 404, (11) all 4 vendor /api/vendor/payables/* endpoints reject with 401/403 without partner token AND reject admin-only JWT (confirms get_partner_user_from_header guard works), (12) duplicate-pending block confirmed via DB-level guard logic, (13) sidebar nav 'Vendor Payables' present at /app/frontend/src/config/adminNavigation.js:51 under Payments & Finance.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout (429) is sensitive — first test run tripped it after ~10 admin logins. Tests now cache the token across the suite (single login). This is correct security behaviour but worth noting for any test authoring.

critical_code_review_comments: vendor_payables_routes.py lines 99-128 _get_vendor_doc: dual-collection lookup (partners by ObjectId OR users by id+role=vendor) is implemented correctly and verified end-to-end.
_set_vendor_modality writes partner_modality_updated_by='admin' for direct admin call, 'admin_approval' when invoked from approve_modality_request — good audit trail.
generate_monthly_statements correctly skips already-paid statements (frozen) and tags vendor_orders with payable_group='statement:<id>' so they don't double-count in /stats and /ledger — verified.
qr_code_routes.py uses PRODUCTION_DOMAIN env if set else FRONTEND_URL else fallback to https://konekt.co.tz — sensible defaults; _frontend_base() works.
Minor: in vendor_payables_routes._get_vendor_doc the partners projection is {_id:0, ...} and then we re-stamp 'id': vendor_id manually — fine, no leak of ObjectId.
Minor (already-pending pattern) in request_modality is correct (DB find_one then 400) — duplicate-block logic verified.
seed_data_creation: Tests seed temporary vendor_modality_requests rows (TEST_dup_vendor + uuid-based) and clean up in finally blocks. Modality flips on a real product partner are reset to pay_per_order at end of each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_session_3a_extended.py as regression. The _admin_token() helper caches the JWT for the suite to avoid the 429 brute-force lockout — keep this pattern. No partner token is available in this pod; partner-only endpoint coverage is limited to the 401/403 guard check + admin-JWT-rejection check. Full vendor-side endpoint testing (POST /vendor/payables/request-modality duplicate 400, /upload-invoice) requires a real partner JWT.
